title: rubus × latidentatus
intro: Rubus × latidentatus, also known as the Hybrid Blackberry or Japanese Blackberry, is a plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is a cross between the black raspberry (Rubus occidentalis) and the Japanese bramble (Rubus lasiophyllus). The leaves and stems of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus × latidentatus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and make it easier to use in herbal preparations.
